title: AtomicUnitV1
description: Post-atomicity-enforcement extraction unit. Complete, self-contained, indivisible ontological component. Pre-typing. Pre-lane. Pre-canonical.
$comment:
AU_FINGERPRINT (design decision — not contract-specified):
Normalization norm(x): trim → lowercase → collapse whitespace to _ → remove disallowed punctuation → null = empty
Concatenation: AU-1.0 | text_norm | parentSourceID_norm | sourceReference_norm
Hash: SHA-256 → lowercase hex
ID format: AU_<sha256hex>

SEEDTYPE INTENTIONALLY ABSENT:
Per GLOBAL-SPLIT-DOCTRINE.md: "Seed: An atomic, post-split structural unit eligible
for classification and routing (typed into exactly one seed category)."
Seed typing produces a new typed seed object downstream.
The AU schema is pre-typing and must remain lane-neutral.
No seedType field is permitted on AU.

ORDERING REQUIREMENT:
Per CREATIVE-LIBRARY-EXTRACTION-AGENT.md: "Atomicity Enforcement must produce
identical AU count, AU ordering, AU textual boundaries."
splitIndex enforces this at schema level. Replay with identical input MUST
produce identical splitIndex values. Ordering drift is a contract violation.

parentSourceID may reference either:
- A PCR parentSourceId (if this AU came from a composite split)
- A REC id directly (if the REC was already atomic — no split occurred)

id.description: Content-derived ID. AU_<sha256hex> of normalized text + parentSourceID + sourceReference. Stable across replays given identical input.

text.description: Atomic clause text. Complete, self-contained, indivisible. No rewriting. No summarization. Verbatim from source.

parentSourceID.description: Pointer to origin. References either a PCR parentSourceId (composite split) or a REC id (already atomic). Required per GLOBAL-SPLIT-DOCTRINE.md.

sourceReference.description: Article or cluster pointer. Archival origin reference. Required per GLOBAL-SPLIT-DOCTRINE.md. Not present on REC — carried by AU only.

splitIndex.description: 0-based position of this AU within its sibling set. Required for deterministic ordering enforcement. AUs from non-composite RECs use splitIndex: 0.
